ddls:
CREATE TABLE "family_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the passenger */,
  "related_passenger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related passenger */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., sibling, parent) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "embarkation_points" (
  "embarkation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each embarkation point */,
  "embarkation_code" TEXT /* Code of the embarkation point (Q, S, C) */,
  "embarkation_name" TEXT /* Name of the embarkation point */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the embarkation point */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("embarkation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "passenger_classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each passenger class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the passenger class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the passenger class */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survival_reasons" (
  "survival_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survival reason */,
  "survival_reason" TEXT /* Reason for passenger survival */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survival_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the age group */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range for the group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nationalities" (
  "nationality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each nationality */,
  "nationality_name" TEXT /* Name of the nationality */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("nationality_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "currencies" (
  "currency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each currency */,
  "currency_code" TEXT /* Currency code (ISO 4217) */,
  "currency_name" TEXT /* Name of the currency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("currency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "boarding_locations" (
  "boarding_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each boarding location */,
  "boarding_name" TEXT /* Name of the boarding location */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the boarding location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("boarding_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "destinations" (
  "destination_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each destination */,
  "destination_name" TEXT /* Name of the destination */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the destination */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("destination_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the passenger being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the passenger data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the passenger data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "families" (
  "family_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each family */,
  "family_name" TEXT /* Name of the family */,
  "family_size" INTEGER /* Size of the family (number of members) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("family_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "groups" (
  "group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each group */,
  "group_name" TEXT /* Name of the group */,
  "group_size" INTEGER /* Size of the group (number of members) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "decks" (
  "deck_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each deck */,
  "deck_name" TEXT /* Name of the deck */,
  "deck_description" TEXT /* Description of the deck */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("deck_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cabins" (
  "cabin_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cabin */,
  "cabin_name" TEXT /* Name of the cabin */,
  "cabin_class" TEXT /* Class of the cabin */,
  "berth" TEXT /* Berth number of the cabin */,
  "deck_id" INTEGER /* ID of the deck the cabin is on */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cabin_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each embarkation point
Code of the embarkation point (Q, S, C)
Unique identifier for each passenger class
Unique identifier for each survival reason
Unique identifier for each age group
Unique identifier for each boarding location
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin)

ID of the user accessing the passenger data
